name: Factory / Shift Worker
clinical_context: Works 8h rotating shifts in manufacturing or industrial environments where fatigue-related errors cause machinery accidents and repetitive strain injuries. Weekly or bi-weekly rotation between day, evening, and night shifts creates chronic circadian disruption. Physical labor accelerates fatigue accumulation compared to sedentary roles.
avoid_patterns: Operating heavy machinery within 2h of waking from day sleep after night shift
Rotating shifts more frequently than once per week
Skipping meals during transition between shift rotations
recovery_notes: Rotating 8h shifts in manufacturing cause slower circadian adaptation than 12h shifts because the body never fully adjusts before rotation occurs. Bright light exposure is the most powerful re-entrainment tool — morning light for day shift, avoiding light after night shift. Meal timing anchors the metabolic circadian clock independently of the light cycle.
